codeReview.summary: The committed changes correctly add the required manifest, placeholder directories, and .gitignore in the new extension project, but the worker handoff misreports the availability of the extension-builder skill and overstates the exact gemini extensions link command that succeeded.

issues.description: The handoff claims the extension-builder skill was unavailable, but /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md exists. This makes the reported deviation inaccurate and weakens trust in the execution record.

The recorded verification command says `echo Y | gemini extensions link .`, but the transcript shows the plain link command first failed because the extension was already installed and the worker then uninstalled/re-linked before validating. The feature outcome is correct, but the verification record is not an exact account of what was required to achieve exit code 0.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker reported that the extension-builder skill was not available even though the project contains .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md. Either the worker procedure for locating project-local skills is unclear, or the execution environment does not surface them consistently.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Project file exists at /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md, while the handoff deviation in 2026-03-12T21-01-52-229Z__extension-manifest-and-structure__4a7cb9a0-3aeb-40b2-b9c8-938083be4e52.json states the skill was not found/available.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml exposes only primitive validators and no single project-level validation command, which contributed to the worker skipping baseline validation and inventing a suggested `test` command for a declarative project.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension/.factory/services.yaml only defines validate-json, validate-extension, validate-toml, and validate-shell; AGENTS.md separately explains that this project has no bun/typecheck workflow.

summary: Reviewed transcript, handoff, commit 38e72b2, and current repo state for extension-manifest-and-structure. The implementation itself satisfies the feature requirements in /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension, but the worker handoff contains inaccurate procedural and verification claims, so this review fails on execution-record fidelity rather than product behavior.
